mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days whenever FEDFUNDS trends upward over the prior 3 months.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d return stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive-slope periods show equal or higher forward returns than negative-slope periods (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises above a 0.3 percentage-point increase over the trailing 3 months, this signals an emerging labor-market deterioration that precedes broader earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive median 60-day forward return in more than 55% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS growth and INDPRO growth signals a synchronized labor-and-production slowdown that individually weak single-series signals fail to capture; joint deceleration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth and INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turn negative simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows no statistically significant difference in mean forward 20d return versus PAYEMS-only or INDPRO-only trigger samples (p>0.10), indicating no incremental interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS elevated and rising), a simultaneous decline in HOUST signals housing-sector stress transmitting into broader demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; outside tightening regimes the same HOUST decline is not predictive.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above its trailing 12-month median AND HOUST falls >5% over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: HOUST decline during non-tightening regimes produces equally negative forward returns as during tightening regimes, or the tightening-conditional sample shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending momentum that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Correlation between RSAFS month-over-month change and forward 5-day return is not distinguishable from zero (|corr|<0.02) over rolling 3-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates above 4%, markets price in a higher probability of aggressive monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples compress in anticipation.
failure_condition: Triggered high-inflation periods show forward 20d returns statistically indistinguishable from or greater than baseline unconditional returns (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising FEDFUNDS rate and a simultaneously falling UNRATE signals an overheating economy prompting the central bank to accelerate tightening beyond market expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days that exceed what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises over trailing 6 months AND UNRATE falls over trailing 6 months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample forward 60d returns are not significantly more negative than the FEDFUNDS-only trigger sample (p>0.10), indicating no interaction premium.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, a rebound in INDPRO signals reaccelerating industrial output that leads equity risk appetite higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days; during stable expansion regimes the same INDPRO uptick carries no incremental signal.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month increase fails to produce forward 5-day returns significantly above baseline during identified post-shock recovery windows (p>0.05), or effect is equally strong in stable expansion regimes.
